concepts.name: Training Cost Asymmetry
concepts.description: The million-to-one resource disparity between training a model and running inference due to repeated forward passes, backward passes, and optimizer steps.

concepts.name: Computational Intensity
concepts.description: The sustained petaFLOPS-scale throughput required by training workloads over long wall-clock durations.

concepts.name: Memory Pressure
concepts.description: The combined storage requirements of model weights, optimizer states, and forward-pass activations that often exceed single-accelerator capacity.

concepts.name: Staged System Pipeline
concepts.description: A coordinated architecture of data loading, host-to-device transfer, computation, and parameter synchronization.

concepts.name: Accelerator Bubbles
concepts.description: Intervals of idle silicon where compute units sit at zero percent utilization waiting for data transfer or synchronization to complete.

concepts.name: Asynchronous Prefetching
concepts.description: Overlapping data loading and preprocessing with accelerator computation to prevent pipeline starvation.

concepts.name: Iron Law of Training
concepts.description: A framework mapping training time performance to total operations, peak hardware throughput, and system utilization.

concepts.name: Mini-batch Processing
concepts.description: Grouping training examples to convert memory-bound vector operations into compute-bound matrix multiplications for better hardware utilization.

concepts.name: Optimizer Memory Overhead
concepts.description: The additional memory required to store mathematical state like momentum and moving averages for parameter updates.

concepts.name: Activation Memory
concepts.description: The storage required to cache forward-pass intermediate values for use during gradient computation in the backward pass.

concepts.name: Arithmetic Intensity
concepts.description: The ratio of floating-point operations to memory bytes accessed, determining whether an operation is constrained by compute or bandwidth.

concepts.name: Roofline Model
concepts.description: A diagnostic framework that maps operations against hardware peak compute and memory bandwidth limits to identify bottlenecks.

concepts.name: Compute-bound Operations
concepts.description: Workloads limited by processor ALU throughput rather than data transfer rates, typically characterized by large matrix multiplications.

concepts.name: Memory-bound Operations
concepts.description: Workloads limited by data movement speed between memory hierarchies rather than compute speed, such as activation functions.

concepts.name: Min-of-Three Relationship
concepts.description: The governing principle that overall system throughput is bottlenecked by the slowest of preprocessing, PCIe transfer, or GPU computation.

concepts.name: Mixed-Precision Training
concepts.description: Utilizing lower precision formats like FP16 or BF16 to double compute throughput and halve memory requirements without sacrificing convergence.

concepts.name: IO-Aware Algorithm Design
concepts.description: Structuring algorithms to minimize data movement across memory hierarchies rather than just minimizing raw FLOP counts.

concepts.name: Tiling Algorithms
concepts.description: Decomposing large matrix computations into smaller blocks that fit entirely within fast on-chip SRAM to minimize HBM round-trips.

concepts.name: Flash Attention
concepts.description: An IO-aware algorithm that computes exact attention using SRAM tiling to prevent materializing quadratic intermediate matrices in slow HBM.

concepts.name: Gradient Accumulation
concepts.description: Simulating larger effective batch sizes by computing and summing gradients across sequential micro-batches before updating parameters.

concepts.name: Activation Checkpointing
concepts.description: Selectively discarding forward-pass activations and recomputing them during backpropagation to trade compute cycles for significant memory savings.

concepts.name: Data Parallelism
concepts.description: A scaling strategy where the entire model is replicated across multiple accelerators that process different data batches and synchronize gradients.

concepts.name: Model Parallelism
concepts.description: A scaling strategy that partitions a single model's layers or operations across multiple accelerators when it exceeds single-device memory limits.

concepts.name: Pipeline Parallelism
concepts.description: Overlapping the execution of partitioned model segments by splitting batches into sequential microbatches to reduce idle time.

concepts.name: Tensor Parallelism
concepts.description: Splitting individual matrix multiplication operations across multiple accelerators to reduce per-device memory load and compute time.
